title: sitobolium smithii
intro: Sitobolium smithii, also known as the sweet grass or buffalo grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in Native American cultures, to treat various health issues such as fever, sore throats, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health. Sitobolium smithii can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional ceremonies and rituals.
